title: anthriscus scabra
intro: Anthriscus scabra, also known as wild chervil or sea chervil, is a biennial herb native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its fern-like leaves and small white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve indigestion and gas. The health benefits of Anthriscus scabra include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system. The plant is commonly prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and can also be used in cooking to add a mild onion-like flavor to dishes.
